title: chelone obliqua subsp. typica
intro: Chelone obliqua subsp. typica, commonly known as the turtlehead, is a plant species within the family Plantaginaceae. The turtlehead plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the turtlehead plant can be dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
